skill.name: ui-design-system
skill.description: UI design system toolkit for generating design tokens, documenting component standards, calculating responsive scales, and preparing developer handoff materials.
skill.summary: Generate practical UI design system guidance and token structures for product teams.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 28 issues, including malware keywords, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and Windows SAM indicators. Manual review found no network access, credential access, shell execution, cryptography, C2 behavior, Windows SAM access, or prompt injection; the keyword alerts are false positives from design text, color values, and output labels. The documented local Python command remains a low-risk community-code execution factor.

low_findings.title: Documented Local Script Invocation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md documents running a bundled Python script from the command line. This is not Ruby or shell backtick execution, but users should review community scripts before running them locally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a direct local Python usage instruction. The command targets a bundled script and does not include dynamic shell construction or remote input.

low_findings.title: Static Malware Keyword Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high and critical static detections map to design metadata, color tokens, typography labels, imports, and output labels. No evidence found for weak cryptography, C2 behavior, Windows SAM access, or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited files shows no malicious operations or security-sensitive APIs. The suspicious strings are ordinary design-system terms, constants, or display text.

low_findings.title: Generator Script Syntax Error
low_findings.description: The warning color string is split across lines, causing Python compilation to fail. This is a reliability defect rather than evidence of malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Python compilation fails at the cited line with an unterminated string literal. The failure prevents execution and does not indicate data access or exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Design teams need consistent tokens, component rules, and responsive standards across products. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured design system assets for design and engineering handoff.
actual_capabilities: Generates color palette structures from a brand color.
Defines typography scales, spacing systems, sizing tokens, and breakpoints.
Creates component documentation guidance for consistent UI implementation.
Supports export concepts for JSON, CSS variables, and SCSS tokens.
Provides responsive design and developer handoff guidance.
limitations: The included Python generator currently has a syntax error and may need correction before execution.
It does not connect directly to Figma, Sketch, or design asset libraries.
Generated tokens still require brand, accessibility, and product review.
It supports only the documented modern, classic, and playful style options.

use_cases.title: Create Brand Token Foundations
use_cases.description: Generate a starter token structure for colors, type, spacing, elevation, and breakpoints.

use_cases.title: Document Component Standards
use_cases.description: Turn design decisions into clear component usage notes for repeated product patterns.

use_cases.title: Prepare Engineering Handoff
use_cases.description: Convert design system rules into implementation-ready guidance for frontend teams.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Starter Tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new brand system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a starter UI design token system for brand color #2563EB using a modern style. Include color, type, spacing, sizing, shadows, breakpoints, and usage notes.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt Tokens To A Product
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a design token plan for a B2B dashboard. Use a calm visual tone, accessible contrast, compact spacing, and clear handoff notes for engineers.

prompt_templates.title: Document Component Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Create design system documentation for buttons, inputs, cards, modals, and notifications. Include states, accessibility requirements, responsive behavior, and implementation guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Design System Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this design system outline for token gaps, inconsistent component rules, accessibility risks, and handoff issues. Recommend prioritized fixes.

output_examples.input: Create tokens for a modern finance dashboard with a blue brand color.
output: A structured palette with primary, secondary, neutral, semantic, and surface color groups.
A practical type scale, spacing system, elevation set, and breakpoint set.
Developer handoff notes that explain naming, usage, and accessibility checks.

output_examples.input: Document button and input standards for a SaaS design system.
output: Component guidance covering sizes, states, labels, validation, and keyboard behavior.
Responsive rules for dense desktop layouts and smaller mobile screens.
A checklist for designer and frontend engineer review.

output_examples.input: Audit an existing design system for handoff quality.
output: A prioritized list of missing tokens and unclear component rules.
Accessibility and responsiveness issues that need product review.
Recommendations for cleaner naming and implementation documentation.

best_practices: Start with brand goals, accessibility requirements, and product density before generating tokens.
Review generated values with designers and engineers before adding them to production systems.
Keep token names stable and document how each token should be used.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated tokens as final brand approval.
Do not add component rules without accessibility states and responsive behavior.
Do not run bundled community scripts before reviewing the source.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create design tokens, component documentation, responsive standards, and developer handoff guidance.

faq.question: Can it export real token files?
faq.answer: The included script is intended to output JSON, CSS, and SCSS formats, but it currently needs a syntax fix before execution.

faq.question: Does it replace a design system team?
faq.answer: No. It creates structured starting points that still need design, accessibility, and engineering review.

faq.question: Does it connect to design tools?
faq.answer: No. It does not include direct integrations with Figma, Sketch, or similar design platforms.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: It is documented for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found no confirmed malicious behavior. The main concern is low-risk local script execution from a community source.
